issues.title: Silent rejection
issues.invariant: Method not allowed must return 405 response directly, not raise HTTPException which bypasses proper status handling

issues.title: Scope mutation timing is a contract with middleware.** Original code mutates scope early so middleware can access path_params. The "redundant" check in handle() is a second
That scope mutation is a message
That **Routes are now HTTP
issues.invariant: Route.matches() now returns three values including HTTP status code and must be fully unpacked

issues.title: That **we've inverted the responsibility model.** Original
Original design
issues.invariant: Duplicate path parameters must be detected and prevented from silently overwriting previous convertor type definitions

issues.title: First improvement
issues.invariant: Request instantiation must be deferred into the exception-handling wrapper to preserve failure diagnostics from Request creation

issues.title: Second improvement
issues.invariant: route.matches() unpacking must handle all 3 return values consistently across all call sites

issues.title: Original
issues.invariant: Nested function names must be distinct from enclosing function names to prevent shadowing and enable correct reference resolution.

issues.title: Inverted
issues.invariant: Input validation must use explicit exception raising, not assert statements which can be disabled at runtime

issues.title: Router
issues.invariant: Route handlers must be visible to exception handlers for policy enforcement

issues.title: Event
issues.invariant: Architecture documentation must accurately describe all design patterns including temporal and behavioral couplings

issues.title: Centralized (Router knows all Routes)
issues.invariant: Router must not require exhaustive knowledge of all possible Route convertor types in advance

issues.title: Route omniscient
issues.invariant: Path parameter keys must be validated to exist in param_convertors before dictionary access.

issues.title: If Match.NONE on one route, but Match.PARTIAL on another, 405 response is delayed; priority unclear
Mount doesn't report allowed methods to parent Router; parent can't return 405 correctly
scope mutated in matches(); if handle() raises exception, scope left in partial state; middleware sees corrupted path_params
Redirect logic triggers on Match.PARTIAL; POST to `/api` redirects to `/api/` (GET), client gets 405 on redirect
Custom convertors can return URL-unsafe strings (e.g., containing "/"); URLs become malformed
issues.invariant: URL parameter values must be URL-encoded to prevent special characters in custom convertor results from malforming URL paths.

issues.title: URL reconstruction doesn't validate parameters against convertor constraints; can generate invalid URLs
Middleware that wraps self.app sees "matched route" even on Match.PARTIAL; can't distinguish matched vs rejected
WebSocket scope handling doesn't apply method-like validation; if middleware tries to enforce per-subtype rules, they're silent-failed
issues.invariant: WebSocket requests must undergo path validation via matches() and return appropriate match status, not silently bypass validation by always returning NONE

issues.title: Duplicate param detection happens at Route.__init__; if routes are lazy-loaded, error is hard to trace
issues.invariant: Duplicate parameter detection must collect all instances before raising, enabling unified error reporting with complete path context instead of aborting on first occurrence

issues.title: If request has URL-encoded slashes (%2F), Mount's path extraction might decode them incorrectly; remaining_path breaks Mount composition
issues.invariant: Path position calculations must use URL-encoded string lengths from the matched regex groups, not decoded parameter lengths, to avoid off-by-one slicing errors when percent-encoded slashes (%2F) are present

issues.title: Adding a new semantic constraint (e.g., "route version must match Accept header") will require either
Extending Match enum (tightens Router
issues.invariant: Match must support comparison operators to enable order-based route dispatch logic without modifying Router when enum is extended.

issues.title: Fixing bug #1 (Mount methods transparency) will require
Allow header has non-deterministic ordering
issues.invariant: HTTP headers must have deterministic values; iterating a set produces arbitrary order

issues.title: Parameter injection in url_path_for via replace_params
issues.invariant: String replacement of template placeholders must not allow one parameter's value to create a placeholder that a subsequent parameter replaces

issues.title: redirect_slashes rstrip removes ALL trailing slashes instead of one
issues.invariant: Slash redirect should toggle one trailing slash, not strip all trailing slashes

issues.title: Inner function 'app' shadows outer function 'app' in request_response
issues.invariant: Nested functions should not shadow enclosing function names to avoid accidental rebinding

issues.title: Mount.matches never returns Match.PARTIAL, breaking cross-mount 405 handling
redirect_slashes issues redirect even for PARTIAL (wrong method) matches
issues.invariant: Slash redirects should only occur when the alternative path fully matches the request, not when it would result in a method-not-allowed error
